experiments.title: How call to action on a Facebook ad during a drive impacts donor conversions

rows.name: NA WUNC Spring Drive 2023 Learn More
NA WUNC Spring Drive 2023 Donate Now

experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by using the Donate Now call to action for Facebook viewers will achieve a higher conversion of donors because the call to action is clear.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 04/27/2023 - 05/04/2023
WUNC is a North Carolina Public Radio Station. During the Spring Drive, we decided to test a Learn More vs Donate Now call to action.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this A/B experiment is that using a clear call to action like "Donate Now" led to a 16.2% increase in purchases for all traffic. However, the statistical level of confidence was only 65.4%, which is below the recommended 85% level, and thus, the results cannot be considered valid. Additionally, the experiment revealed that using "Donate Now" led to a significant increase in Cost per Click, with a 100% level of confidence. This indicates that while this clear call to action may lead to more conversions, it may also lead to higher costs.

In the future, WUNC should conduct experiments with larger sample sizes and higher levels of statistical significance before making any definitive decisions about changing their call to action. They should also consider the tradeoff between higher conversion rates and increased costs. It may be helpful to explore other variations of the call to action, such as "Donate Today" or "Make a Donation," to see if they lead to similar conversion rates without significantly increasing costs. Overall, this experiment demonstrates the importance of using data and statistical significance to validate results before making any changes to marketing strategies.

experiments.title: How invitational language on a landing page impacted conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by having language that is more invitational for potential acquisition individuals will achieve a higher conversion rate because the copy is more invitational and things to do with family and friends .
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 02/22/2023 - 03/30/2023
WUNC is a North Carolina Public Radio news and radio station.
WUNC is currently running an acquisition offer around Things To Do in North Carolina. In an effort to drive conversion rates, we decided to test a more invitational language approach.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The A/B experiment conducted by WUNC with the hypothesis that an invitational language approach would yield higher conversion rates for email acquisition did not produce a conclusive result as the treatment showed a 4.9% decrease in email acquisition compared to the control group. However, the statistical level of confidence was only 70.6%, below the recommended confidence level of 85%, hence, the results cannot be considered valid for future decision making.

Despite this, the experiment identified a significant increase in email acquisition from Desktop visitors, with a 193% increase and a 95% level of confidence. This result suggests that future experimentation should focus on optimizing user experience for Desktop visitors specifically, perhaps by creating a more tailored approach to their specific user requirements.

Overall, the key learning from this experiment is the importance of having a sufficiently high level of statistical confidence to make valid decisions based on the experiment’s results. Moreover, it is crucial to carefully consider the target audience to optimize any experiment, as seen with Desktop users in this experiment. Thus, future experiments with invitational language should be run with a larger audience and a better targeted approach to maximize the benefit of such an approach.

experiments.title: How a headline on a donation page impacted conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by adding a headline for donation page viewers will achieve a higher conversion rate because their is clarity on how their gift will be used.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 02/15/2023 - 03/30/2023
WUNC is a North Carolina Public Radio station. For their main donation page, we decided to test adding a headline to the page to drive clarity.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The A/B experiment conducted on the donation page of WUNC, a nonprofit North Carolina Public Radio station, aimed to test the impact of a headline on the donation page. The hypothesis was that adding a headline would clarify how the gift would be used and increase the conversion rate. The experiment ran for 42 days and showed that the treatment had a 4.0% increase in revenue for all traffic, but with a statistical level of confidence of only 57.5%, it cannot be considered valid for future decision-making.

However, there were some significant findings from the experiment that should be taken into account for future experiments. Tablet visitors saw a 5922% increase in revenue, with a 98% level of confidence. On the other hand, organic search visitors experienced a 570% decrease in revenue, with a 96% level of confidence.

The key learning from this experiment is that adding a headline to the donation page can have a positive impact on revenue. However, more research is needed to determine the specific language and message that resonates with different visitor segments. It is also important to note that statistical significance is crucial in making data-driven decisions and cannot be ignored. Future experiments should aim for a higher level of statistical confidence before implementing changes.

experiments.title: How dual donate buttons impacted donations
experiments.researchQuestion: Will adding a second donate button increase donor conversions?
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/28/2022 - 12/23/2022
WUNC is an NPR public news and radio station based in North Carolina.
WUNC has a large number of yearly web traffic but very few become donors. In an effort to convert more web visitors into donors, we decided to test a dual donate button.
Our hypothesis is that by adding a second donate button calling out becoming a monthly donor, we will see an increase in conversions, specifically monthly donors, due to the clarity of the ask.
We tested the dual donate button across the wunc.org site.
experiments.keyLearnings:
For this experiment, we could not validate the response as we only saw a 2.8% decrease in conversions at a 68.8% level of confidence for total donations.

For our recurring donor segment, we saw a 5.6% increase in donations with a confidence level of 34.4% so that segment did not see an impact either.

We are looking into alternate tests to encourage web traffic to convert. Future tests include a sticky donate button, dear readers, and content offered within articles.

experiments.title: How a headline on a donation page impacted conversions
experiments.researchQuestion: We believe that by adding a headline on the main donation form for all traffic will achieve a higher conversion rate because there is a clear call to action and reason for the donation.
experiments.summary:
Timeframe: 11/10/2023 - 11/17/2023
WUNC is North Carolina’s largest NPR station.
In an effort to convert donors, we decided to test adding a headline to WUNC’s main donation page during their Fall Drive. All traffic from email, advertising and web point to this page.
We did experience a delay in getting the experiment live due to technical issues, so we did miss the first 4 days of the drive which historically have the most traffic.
experiments.keyLearnings:
The key learning from this experiment is that adding a headline to the main donation form for all traffic did not achieve a higher conversion rate. In fact, the treatment group experienced a decrease in donations compared to the control group. However, due to technical issues and missing the first 4 days of the drive which historically have the most traffic, the results cannot be considered valid.
In future experimentation, it is important to ensure that technical issues are resolved promptly to avoid missing significant portions of the experiment. Additionally, it would be beneficial to conduct experiments for a longer duration to gather more accurate and comprehensive data.
Based on the inconclusive results of this experiment, it would be wise to test other variations and elements on the main donation page to identify factors that may lead to a higher conversion rate. It is essential to continue experimenting and iterating on the donation page to optimize its effectiveness in converting donors.
